summary: Implements 9 shared type definitions in types/, 4 condition helpers in conditions/, 4 config rendering functions in config/, 3 pipeline/plugin functions in plugins/, and 3 policy functions in policy/. All 14 new files have correct SPDX headers and Feature: CC-0004 tracking. Tests pass with 97-100% coverage across all packages. go vet and golangci-lint report zero issues. Three issues require changes: (1) InjectOsloPolicyConfig missing empty-path no-op guard per spec, (2) renderSortedINI duplicates config.RenderINI unnecessarily, (3) policy/doc.go missing Feature tracking comment in the conventional location.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist for all 13 exported functions
Coverage meets 80% target (conditions: 100%, config: 100%, plugins: 97.9%, policy: 97.4%)
Edge cases covered: nil inputs, empty maps, boundary values
Immutability tests for MergeDefaults, InjectSecrets, RenderPluginConfig, MergePolicies
Table-driven test structure with gomega dot-import and NewGomegaWithT(t)
Missing test: InjectOsloPolicyConfig with empty policyFilePath (no-op case)
Deterministic output verified for RenderINI, RenderPolicyYAML, RenderPastePipelineINI

code_quality_checklist.item: Functions are small and focused (Single Responsibility)
No dead code or commented-out code
Max nesting levels appropriate (guard clauses used)

security_checklist.item: All functions are pure (no K8s API calls, no file I/O, no network)
No injection vulnerabilities (INI rendering uses simple string formatting)
No sensitive data in logs or errors

architecture_checklist.item: Package structure matches plan (types, conditions, config, plugins, policy)
SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers on all files
Feature: CC-0004 tracking on all files
Pure functions with no side effects (except InjectOsloPolicyConfig mutates in-place as documented)
Types use corev1.LocalObjectReference for cluster refs
DatabaseSpec, MessagingSpec, CacheSpec document mutual exclusivity via doc comments

dry_yagni_checklist.item: renderSortedINI in plugins duplicates config.RenderINI without need (no circular import exists)
RenderPastePipelineINI is an extra convenience function beyond spec (acceptable, tested)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty/nil inputs handled with early returns
ValidatePolicyRules returns errors for invalid inputs

defensive_checklist.item: Nil map inputs treated as empty maps throughout
MergeDefaults, InjectSecrets, MergePolicies return new maps (no input mutation)

architecture_findings.description: conditions/ implements SetCondition/GetCondition directly instead of delegating to k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta helpers as spec describes. Implementation is functionally correct and well-tested. The custom implementation manages LastTransitionTime correctly (preserves on same status, updates on change). Acceptable deviation — reduces dependency surface and the functions are simple enough to self-implement reliably.
architecture_findings.fix: No change required. Document the intentional deviation if consumers later need meta.SetStatusCondition behavior (e.g. ObservedGeneration handling).

architecture_findings.description: PipelinePosition implemented as string enum (before/after) instead of struct with After/Before string fields as spec describes. The spec says 'PipelinePosition is a struct with After/Before string fields (derived from CRD YAML position: { after: authtoken })' which implies positioning relative to a named filter. Current implementation only supports 'before all base filters' or 'after all base filters'. This is simpler and sufficient for current tests, but may need revision when operators need granular positioning (e.g. 'insert after authtoken but before sizelimit').
architecture_findings.fix: Acceptable for B001 foundation. When S013 (reconcilers) needs granular positioning, PipelinePosition can be changed to a struct since there are no consumers yet.

architecture_findings.description: MergePolicies signature takes (base, override types.PolicySpec) instead of spec's (inline, external map[string]string). ValidatePolicyRules takes types.PolicySpec and returns error instead of spec's (rules map[string]string) field.ErrorList. These are intentional design improvements: MergePolicies handles ConfigMapRef merging; ValidatePolicyRules validates the full PolicySpec including ConfigMapRef presence. The error return (vs field.ErrorList) means only the first validation error is reported, but since CRD webhook validation (S011) will use CEL markers, this utility function's simpler error return is acceptable.
architecture_findings.fix: No change required. If S011 webhook validation needs field.ErrorList, a separate webhook-oriented validator can be added.

issues_found.description: InjectOsloPolicyConfig missing empty-path guard. Spec (task 2.2) explicitly states: 'if policyFilePath is non-empty, ensure oslo_policy section exists and set policy_file key; if empty, no-op'. Current implementation always injects, even with empty string, which would create an oslo_policy section with policy_file = '' — incorrect behavior that could cause oslo.policy to fail loading.
issues_found.fix: Add early return: if policyFilePath == "" { return }. Add test case: {name: "empty path is no-op", config: map[string]map[string]string{"DEFAULT": {"debug": "true"}}, policyFilePath: "", want: map[string]map[string]string{"DEFAULT": {"debug": "true"}}}.

issues_found.description: Missing test for InjectOsloPolicyConfig empty-path no-op edge case. All other functions have nil/empty edge case tests, but InjectOsloPolicyConfig is missing the empty policyFilePath case that the spec requires to be a no-op.
issues_found.fix: Add table-driven test case for empty policyFilePath that verifies config is unchanged.

issues_found.description: renderSortedINI in plugins/plugins.go:102-131 is a verbatim copy of config.RenderINI (config/config.go:22-51). The comment says 'avoids a circular import' but there is no circular dependency: plugins imports types, config has no imports from plugins. plugins can safely import config and call config.RenderINI.
issues_found.fix: Replace renderSortedINI with a call to config.RenderINI: import the config package, change RenderPastePipelineINI to call config.RenderINI(sections), and delete the renderSortedINI function.

issues_found.description: policy/doc.go embeds Feature ID in the doc comment text ('(CC-0004)') instead of using the standalone '// Feature: CC-0004' comment line that all other packages use. While the Feature ID is present, it doesn't follow the established convention.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a test case for ValidatePolicyRules with multiple empty values to verify behavior (currently only first error is returned — acceptable but worth documenting)
The conditions package could benefit from a constant for the 'Ready' condition type string to avoid magic string usage across the codebase (e.g. const ConditionReady = "Ready")
next_steps: Fix C3-1: Add empty-path guard to InjectOsloPolicyConfig and corresponding test
Fix Q3-1: Replace renderSortedINI with config.RenderINI import
Fix D1-1: Align policy/doc.go Feature tracking comment with convention
Re-run go test and golangci-lint to verify fixes

detected_patterns.name: Immutability verification test for map-returning pure functions
detected_patterns.applies_when: Writing a pure function that accepts map inputs and returns a new map (i.e., must not mutate inputs)
detected_patterns.description: Every pure function that takes map parameters and returns a new map has a dedicated _doesNotMutateInput(s) test. The test calls the function, then mutates the returned map, and asserts the original input maps are unchanged. This ensures the function properly deep-copies map values rather than sharing references. Applied consistently across 4 functions in 3 packages: MergeDefaults, InjectSecrets, RenderPluginConfig, MergePolicies.
